tablex = 2.5
tabley = -2
tablez = -1
tableLength=4
tableWidth=5
tableHeight=1
legRadius=tableWidth/10
tableColor=color.black
scene.background=color.white
scene.range=5
table=box(pos=vector(tablex, tabley, tablez), color=tableColor, length=tableLength, width=tableWidth, height=tableHeight)
cylinder(color=table.color, radius=legRadius, axis=vector(0, -tableHeight*4, 0), pos=table.pos)
MyPrograms__PlanetaryMotion.py:
scene.title = "Modeling the motion of planets with the gravitational force"
scene.height = 600
scene.width = 800
planet1 = sphere(pos=vector(1,0,0), radius=0.05, color=color.green, mass = 1, momentum=vector(0,30,0), make_trail=True)

planet2 = sphere(pos=vector(0,3,0), radius=0.075, color=vector(0.0,0.82,0.33), mass = 2, momentum=vector(-35,0,0), make_trail=True)
                  
planet3  = sphere(pos=vector(0,-4,0), radius=0.1, color=vector(0.58,0.153,0.68), mass = 10, momentum=vector(160,0,0), make_trail=True)

star = sphere(pos=vector(0,0,0), radius=0.2, color=color.yellow, mass = 2.0*1000, momentum=vector(0,0,0), make_trail=True)
               
def gravitationalForce(p1,p2):
    G = 1 #real-world value is : G = 6.67e-11
    rVector = p1.pos - p2.pos
    rMagnitude = mag(rVector)
    rHat = rVector / rMagnitude
    F = - rHat * G * p1.mass * p2.mass /rMagnitude**2
    return F
    
t = 0
dt = 0.0001 #The step size.

while (True):
    rate(500)
    
     #Calculte the force using gravitationalForce function
    star.force = gravitationalForce(star,planet1)+gravitationalForce(star,planet2)+gravitationalForce(star,planet3)
    planet1.force = gravitationalForce(planet1,star)+gravitationalForce(planet1,planet2)+gravitationalForce(planet1,planet3)
    planet2.force = gravitationalForce(planet2,star)+gravitationalForce(planet2,planet1)+gravitationalForce(planet2,planet3)
    planet3.force = gravitationalForce(planet3,star)+gravitationalForce(planet3,planet1)+gravitationalForce(planet3,planet2)

    #Update momentum, position and time
    star.momentum = star.momentum + star.force*dt
    planet1.momentum = planet1.momentum + planet1.force*dt
    planet2.momentum = planet2.momentum + planet2.force*dt
    planet3.momentum = planet3.momentum + planet3.force*dt

    star.pos = star.pos + star.momentum/star.mass*dt
    planet1.pos = planet1.pos + planet1.momentum/planet1.mass*dt
    planet2.pos = planet2.pos + planet2.momentum/planet2.mass*dt
    planet3.pos = planet3.pos + planet3.momentum/planet3.mass*dt
    
    t += dt
